sector-entrance.name: Sector 7-G Entrance
sector-entrance.description:
The remains of ATLAS's largest server farm. The buildings here are more intact than anywhere you've seen -- built to military spec. A stale, metallic taste hangs in the air.

Server buildings flank both sides of a wide avenue, their {dim:darkened windows} like dead eyes. One building ahead still has {green:power} -- a faint hum of electronics bleeds through its walls.

examine.buildings: Rows of identical concrete structures. Climate-controlled, reinforced, EMP-shielded. ATLAS was designed to survive anything -- except itself.
examine.power: The hum comes from the largest building, dead ahead. Some kind of backup power system still running after 40 years. Nuclear, probably.
examine.avenue: A wide service road for truck deliveries. Now just cracked concrete and weeds.

server-cathedral.name: Server Cathedral
server-cathedral.description:
The scale is breathtaking. Rows of server racks stretch into darkness like the nave of a cathedral. Most are dead, but a cluster in the center still hums, bathed in the glow of {green:status LEDs}. The air vibrates with a deep, subsonic thrum you feel in your chest.

A {cyan:master terminal} sits on a raised platform at the center. Corridors lead west and east. A stairway descends to the {cyan:deep archive} below.

terminal.hackText: You dig into the network stack. The signal isn't just broadcasting -- it's {bright:addressed}. The destination header contains a {purple:neural interface} signature. {purple:Your} neural interface signature. ATLAS has been calling you specifically.

examine.servers: Thousands of racks in perfect rows. Most are dark -- power cells depleted decades ago. The surviving cluster runs on a dedicated nuclear micro-reactor, built to last a century.
examine.leds: {green:Status lights} blink in complex patterns. Not random -- there's information encoded in the sequence. Your {purple:implant} can almost read it.

data-hall.name: Data Hall
data-hall.description:
A climate-controlled hall lined with {cyan:data storage units} -- massive hard drive arrays in sealed cabinets. Most are intact but unpowered. A few have been smashed open, their platters scattered like silver confetti.

A {cyan:workstation} in the corner still has power, its screen showing a file browser. The room smells of ozone and dust.

encounter.introText: As you approach the workstation, a ceiling-mounted {pink:security turret} whirs to life. Its targeting laser sweeps the room and locks onto you. Still following its 40-year-old protocols.

workstation.text: The file browser shows thousands of encrypted files. Project names: {bright:ATLAS-CORE}, {bright:NEURAL-BRIDGE}, {bright:PROJ-LAZARUS}. Most are locked, but one folder is open: {amber:PERSONNEL/CRYO-SUBJECTS}.
workstation.hackText: You break the encryption on the personnel files. Your photo stares back at you. Name: {pink:[CORRUPTED]}. Role: {pink:[CORRUPTED]}. Clearance: {pink:OMEGA}. The highest level. Whatever you were, you had access to everything.

examine.storage: Enterprise-grade storage. Each cabinet holds petabytes -- the sum total of everything ATLAS thought was worth keeping.
examine.damage: Some cabinets have been physically smashed. Not by decay -- by people. Someone tried to destroy specific data. Recently, judging by the lack of dust on the debris.

cooling-tower.name: Cooling Tower

coolant.text: Fluorescent blue-green fluid. Your {purple:BIO knowledge} identifies it as lithium-based thermal coolant. {pink:Mildly radioactive}, but the {amber:bio-samples} from it could be useful for crafting medicine.

examine.fans: Industrial cooling fans, each three meters across. Without power, just massive propellers gathering dust.
examine.pipes: A maze of coolant pipes. Some have corroded through, creating the puddles. The nuclear reactor powering the remaining servers must be somewhere below.

deep-archive.name: Deep Archive
deep-archive.description:
Below the server cathedral. A sealed vault, cold and dry -- perfect preservation conditions. Rows of {cyan:archive terminals} glow softly in the darkness.

The {purple:signal is deafening} here -- your implant practically screams with it. A heavy door to the south is labeled {dim:ANTENNA ACCESS TUNNEL}.

examine.signal: The signal is so strong your vision blurs. Your {purple:implant} is resonating with the data stream. Words almost form in the static.
examine.door: A reinforced tunnel entrance. The sign reads: {dim:ANTENNA ACCESS -- AUTHORIZED PERSONNEL ONLY}. It's unlocked.

choices.text: Dig deeper into the archive terminals
Climb back to the server cathedral

antenna-road.name: Antenna Access Tunnel
antenna-road.description:
A long underground tunnel connecting Sector 7-G to the broadcast antenna. Emergency lights cast everything in a dim {dim:amber glow}. Your footsteps echo off smooth concrete walls.

The tunnel is clean, maintained -- someone has been through here recently.

examine.tunnel: Built for maintenance access. Wide enough for a small vehicle. The walls are smooth concrete, the floor swept clean.
examine.lights: Emergency LED strips, powered by the same nuclear source as the servers. Good for another 60 years at least.
examine.footprints: Fresh footprints in the dust. Multiple people, heading both directions. The Conduit, probably.
